chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Cr. Bail Application No.S- 650 of 2025 Date Order with signature of Judge Fresh case 1. For orders on MA No.3848/2025 (U/A) 2. For orders on MA No.3849/2025 (Ex.A) 3. For orders on MA No.3850/2025 (561-A CrPC) 4. For hearing of Bail Application 31.07.2025 M/s Ali Gul Abbasi and Muhammad Zohaib Azam Rajput, Advocate for the Applicant/accused. >>>>>..<<<<< 1. Granted. 2. Granted subject to all just exceptions. 3&4. Through this bail application, applicant/accused, namely Muhammad Yaseen S/o Kouro Khan Indhar, seeks protective bail in Crime No.314 of 2024 of P.S Shaheed Murtaza Mirani, Khairpur for offence under Section 302 PPC. It is, inter-alia, contended that the applicant/accused is innocent and there is no any reasonable ground to believe that the applicant has committed any offence as alleged in the FIR and he has falsely been implicated in this case with ulterior motives; that after investigation the applicant/accused was found innocent by the Investigating Officer; that the applicant/accused is ready to surrender before the court concerned along with an application u/s 498 Cr.P.C but apprehends his arrest at the hands of police as the court concerned has issued NBWs against the applicant for his arrest. Without touching merits of the case, protective bail for a period of ten days is granted to applicant/accused subject to his furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/- (Rupees Fifty thousand) and P.R. bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the Additional Registrar of this Court. The operation of NBWs, if any, shall remain suspended for the aforesaid period.

Without touching merits of the case, protective bail for a period of ten days is granted to applicant/accused subject to his furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/- (Rupees Fifty thousand) and P.R. bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the Additional Registrar of this Court. The operation of NBWs, if any, shall remain suspended for the aforesaid period. This order shall cease to have its effect after expiry of aforesaid period or whenever applicant/accused surrenders before the Court concerned, which one occurs earlier. It is, however, made clear that in case the applicant/accused fails to surrender before the Court concerned within stipulated period, his bail bond shall stand automatically forfeited. Criminal Bail Application stands disposed of in the above terms along with listed application. Judge ARBROHI
